phases.title: Phase 1: Java Mastery & Aptitude Foundation
phases.description: Months 1–3 (Weeks 1–12): Java mastery + daily aptitude habit. Build a direct written round speed-advantage.

phases.title: Phase 2: Full DSA & Core CS Subjects
phases.description: Months 4–9 (Weeks 13–36): Master full A–Z DSA (Striver) + DBMS (Gate Smashers) + OS + OOP Deep Dive. Solve 100+ LeetCode Mediums.

phases.title: Phase 3: Competitive Coding & 3 Real Projects
phases.description: Months 10–13.5 (Weeks 37–54): Rated Codeforces/CodeChef sprints + build 3 high-quality Zoho-relevant Java/Spring REST backends.

phases.title: Phase 4: Zoho Final Prep & Mock Simulations
phases.description: Months 14–18 (Weeks 55–72): Re-solve past Zoho machine coding (L3), Top 50 SQL, Pramp mock interviews, and earn Zoho Learn Certifications.

topics.title: OOPs Design Principles & JVM Internals
topics.description: Inheritance, encapsulation, runtime polymorphism, virtual method dispatch, garbage collection heaps, and stack frames.

topics.title: JDK Collections & Hashing Mechanics
topics.description: Deep dive into HashMap collision resolution treeification, ArrayList capacity growth formulas, LinkedList, and concurrent structures.

topics.title: Java Multi-Threading & Exceptions
topics.description: Thread lifecycle, Runnable vs Thread, synchronized blocks, wait/notify, ReentrantLock, and customException pools.

topics.title: Arrays, Sorting, Two-Pointers & Binary Search
topics.description: Fast-slow pointers, sliding window, binary search variations, Dutch National Flag, and Merge Sort variants.

topics.title: Stack, Queue, Recursion & Linked Lists
topics.description: Custom singly & doubly linked lists, queue simulations using arrays, and monotonic stacks.

topics.title: Trees, Heaps, Graphs & DP
topics.description: Solving backtracking grids, BSTs, level BFS, Dijkstra, and tabulations.

topics.title: RDBMS Normalization & SQL Queries
topics.description: Decompositions, JOINS, GROUP BY, Window functions, and indexes optimization.

topics.title: OS Scheduling, Threading & Page Memory
topics.description: Explain scheduling policies, virtual memory sizing, Bankers deadlock, and memory allocation segments.

topics.title: REST Backend Project 1: Contacts Server
topics.description: Spring Boot CRUD routes, SQL database connection, JPA query drivers, and testing.

topics.title: REST Backend Project 2: Inventory Tracker
topics.description: Database schemas, models, complex updates sequences, and GitHub profiling.

topics.title: REST Backend Project 3: Showpiece Helpdesk
topics.description: Premium JWT security logins, API pagination, and server architectures.

topics.title: Railway Reservation System (L3)
topics.description: Manage berth counts (lower, middle, upper, RAC, waiting), booking priority, cancellations, chart configurations.

topics.title: Taxi Dispatching Booking (L3)
topics.description: Manage 4+ taxis, customer booking triggers, nearest distance coordinates calculation, revenue metrics tracker.

topics.title: Mocks, Resume Polish & Certifications
topics.description: Live Pramp peer-to-peer technical interview mock assessments and corporate culture STAR answers.

milestones.title: SOLID Master

milestones.title: Sliding Window Pro
milestones.description: Solve 15 Sliding window questions on LC

milestones.title: Railway Prototype
milestones.description: Simulate ticket booking loop with RAC logic

studySessions.notes: Exploration of Class loaders, Java Stack allocations and Heap segments. Set up standard garbage logger.
Solved work equivalence equations and relative downstream boat rates.
Coded dynamic sliding window trackers. Solved 4 Medium issues related to subarray constraints.
Architected custom SOLID Parking Lot system. Designed interface separations for vehicle size constraints.
Diagrammed resize index hash treeification on JDK 8 hashmap structures.
Investigated dutch-flag partitioning speed records under low auxiliary space conditions.
Completed matrix index circles and blood relative connections challenges.
Worked on simulated deadlock conditions and thread monitor locks.

notes.title: Optimized Sliding Window Concept
Zoho Past Round: Railway Reservation Breakdown
notes.content:
## Zoho Railway Round Notes

### Core Rules Required:
1. **Total Berths**: 63 (e.g. Upper: 21, Middle: 21, Lower: 21).
2. **RAC Count**: 18. **Waiting List (WL)**: 10.
3. **Seat allocation logic**:
   - Lower berth holds priority for Senior Citizens (>60 yrs) and Ladies.
   - If lower is empty/vacated, dispatch to priority.
   - If all berths full, book RAC (Side-lower seats shared by two persons).
   - If RAC full, allocate WL.
4. **Cancellation Rules**:
   - WL cancellation pulls up next WL.
   - RAC cancellation promotes next RAC to confirmed berth, next WL shifts to RAC.
